target.name: Stellaria

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Stellaria is a seed-stage nuclear energy company that has raised €23M, founded by Nicolas Breton and backed by CEA and Schneider Electric. The nuclear energy sector comprises 11 comparable firms: 3 public entities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Standard peer-median valuation is not applicable here. The nuclear energy peer set, while populated with 8 private firms, lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tier characteristics required for robust multiples-based analysis. Specifically, zero same-stage peers and only one adjacent-stage peer exist within the narrow filtering criteria; a minimum of three datapoints is required to establish meaningful medians. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable bias given Stellaria's specialized position in frontier nuclear technology.

Without triangulation through peer multiples, valuation support must derive from alternative methods not present in the current dataset—such as discounted cash flow projections, venture capital method returns analysis, or precedent transaction data within the nuclear sector. The €23M raised to date provides a floor reference but does not establish fair value absent additional methodological inputs. Key uncertainties include the company's development stage within nuclear commercialization, capital intensity of path to revenue, and regulatory pathway risks inherent to the sector. Further analysis would require capital efficiency metrics, technology readiness indicators, or management track record data not available in the current brief.
